story.iWant: services to share common dependencies efficiently
story.soThat: I avoid duplicate resource allocation and ensure consistency
story.tasks: - [ ] Task 1: Extend DI container with dependency scoping capabilities (AC: 1) - [ ] Implement Singleton scope for shared instances across service lifetime - [ ] Implement Scoped scope for shared instances within request/operation context - [ ] Implement Transient scope for new instances per resolution request - [ ] Add scope validation and enforcement in dependency registration - [ ] Task 2: Implement automatic dependency sharing logic (AC: 2) - [ ] Enhance container resolution to check existing singleton instances before creation - [ ] Add dependency sharing detection for common framework dependencies - [ ] Implement smart sharing for repository interfaces, configuration providers, loggers - [ ] Add dependency sharing metrics and monitoring - [ ] Task 3: Ensure thread safety for shared dependencies (AC: 3) - [ ] Implement concurrent access protection for singleton dependency resolution - [ ] Add thread-safe dependency instance storage with minimal locking overhead - [ ] Validate shared dependency implementations are thread-safe (repositories, configs) - [ ] Implement performance tests for concurrent dependency access under load - [ ] Task 4: Implement dependency health monitoring and propagation (AC: 4) - [ ] Add health check interface for dependencies with status propagation - [ ] Implement dependency health aggregation across all dependent services - [ ] Add automatic service notification when shared dependencies become unavailable - [ ] Implement circuit breaker patterns for unhealthy shared dependencies - [ ] Task 5: Optimize memory usage through efficient sharing (AC: 5) - [ ] Add memory usage tracking for shared vs non-shared dependency patterns - [ ] Implement dependency pooling for expensive resources (database connections) - [ ] Add memory profiling tools to validate sharing effectiveness - [ ] Optimize dependency reference management to prevent memory leaks - [ ] Task 6: Implement centralized lifecycle management (AC: 6) - [ ] Add dependency startup ordering based on dependency graph analysis - [ ] Implement proper shutdown sequencing (dependents stopped before dependencies) - [ ] Add dependency lifecycle event broadcasting for monitoring - [ ] Ensure graceful handling of dependency startup/shutdown failures - [ ] Task 7: Enable dependency update propagation (AC: 7) - [ ] Implement dependency update notification system for configuration changes - [ ] Add hot-reload support for shared configuration dependencies - [ ] Implement dependency versioning for safe update propagation - [ ] Add dependency change validation to prevent breaking service contracts

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. **Dependency Scoping**: DI container supports Singleton, Scoped, and Transient dependency lifecycles for efficient resource sharing 2. **Automatic Sharing**: Multiple services automatically share singleton dependencies (database clients, configuration, logging) without explicit coordination 3. **Concurrent Access Safety**: Shared dependencies support concurrent access from multiple services with thread-safe operations and zero contention 4. **Dependency Health Monitoring**: Shared dependency health affects all dependent services with automatic propagation and failover support 5. **Memory Optimization**: Dependency sharing eliminates duplicate resource allocation and enables efficient memory usage across service hierarchies 6. **Lifecycle Management**: Shared dependencies have centralized lifecycle management with proper startup/shutdown ordering 7. **Update Propagation**: Configuration changes and dependency updates propagate to all dependent services automatically

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: FR18: Services can share common dependencies without coupling to implementations. Framework eliminates hard-coded singleton dependencies and supports both constructor injection and factory patterns.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Interface-based registration with type-safe resolution using Go generics, lifecycle management (Singleton, Transient, Scoped), zero runtime reflection overhead, circular dependency detection.

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: Implement dependency scoping in DI container: Singleton, Scoped, Transient. Add dependency health checking and automatic failover. Support dependency proxying for thread safety.

artifact.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Enhanced container with dependency scoping, SharedDependencyManager with singleton/scoped cache, HealthChecker interface for dependency monitoring and lifecycle coordination.

artifact.title: Story 3.2 Implementation
artifact.snippet: Service composition patterns demonstrate effective dependency sharing across embedded services. Dependency validation patterns provide template for shared dependency health checking and lifecycle management.

artifact.reason: Core DI container implementation that needs enhancement with Scoped dependencies and health monitoring
Current scope definitions (Singleton, Transient, Scoped) that need full implementation for Scoped lifecycle
Container interface that needs enhancement with health monitoring and lifecycle management methods
Service composition implementation that demonstrates dependency sharing patterns for embedded services
Core service implementation that will benefit from shared dependency patterns
Service interfaces that define contracts for dependency injection and shared resource usage
Existing test demonstrating singleton behavior that provides pattern for shared dependency testing

story-context.constraints: - **Performance**: Dependency resolution must remain < 1μs for singletons, < 5μs for scoped dependencies as specified in Epic 2 foundation - **Thread Safety**: Zero contention for concurrent dependency access through optimized locking strategies and minimal shared state - **Memory Efficiency**: Eliminate duplicate dependency allocation through intelligent sharing without introducing memory leaks - **Interface Contracts**: Shared dependency enhancements must maintain all existing Container interface contracts and method signatures - **Epic Integration**: Must seamlessly integrate with service embedding from Story 3.2 and middleware pipeline from Story 3.1 - **Backward Compatibility**: Existing Singleton and Transient scope behavior must remain unchanged during Scoped scope addition

interface.name: HealthChecker
SharedDependencyManager
RegisterScoped
interface.signature: func (c *Container) RegisterScoped[T any](impl T, scope DependencyScope) error

interface.name: DependencyScope

tests.standards: Testing follows Epic 1 patterns with comprehensive unit tests using testify/mock for dependency behavior verification. Integration tests validate shared dependency lifecycle with real MongoDB connections. Build tags separate unit tests (//go:build unit) from integration tests (//go:build integration) for fast CI/CD execution. Performance benchmarks ensure dependency resolution targets are met under concurrent load.
tests.locations: sdk/di/*_test.go for container enhancement tests, sdk/health/*_test.go for health monitoring tests, test/ directory for integration tests with shared dependencies across service compositions
tests.ideas: - AC1: Test Scoped dependency lifecycle with request context boundaries and proper cleanup - AC2: Test automatic sharing detection for common framework dependencies (repositories, configs, loggers) - AC3: Test concurrent dependency resolution under high load with 1000+ goroutines to validate zero contention - AC4: Test dependency health monitoring with circuit breaker patterns and service failure propagation - AC5: Test memory usage optimization with before/after profiling to validate sharing effectiveness - AC6: Test centralized lifecycle management with complex dependency graphs and shutdown ordering - AC7: Test configuration update propagation with hot-reload scenarios and service notification patterns
